data.title: Sharp increases in speculative trading signal above-average returns followed by downturns – GS
data.description:
Sharp increases in speculative trading activity historically signal above-average near-term equity returns but poor medium-term prospects, according to Goldman Sachs.

When such trading surges occur, they typically bring slightly higher near-term volatility and often lead to eventual market drawdowns. This pattern has been especially pronounced during the recent period, with the magnitude of the current rise exceeding half the threshold that typically defines “sharp increases.”

 

 

 

Speculative trading activity in the U.S. equity market (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI) has risen sharply. Goldman Sachs’s Speculative Trading Indicator shows high-risk activity has reached the top quintile of levels recorded since 1990, with trading increasingly centered on high-multiple stocks.

“Today, that EV/sales multiple equals 8x, the highest level in recent decades outside of 2000 and 2021,” said Ben Snider, senior U.S. portfolio strategist at Goldman Sachs.

Social media sentiment and retail trader activity have played significant roles in the current market dynamics. Goldman’s Social Media Economic Sentiment Index “has surged from negative territory in April to the highest level since at least 2018 earlier this month,” Snider said. This improvement in sentiment has coincided with a rally in stocks popular with retail traders, similar to patterns observed in 2021.

Stocks with the highest share of trading volumes in the past month include BigBear.ai (BBAI), Lucid Group (LCID), Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA), and Plug Power (PLUG), among others.

In addition, despite the S&P 500 (SP500), (NYSEARCA:SPY) sitting at record highs, current positioning data indicates neutral positioning across U.S. equity investors, he said.

Short interest for the median S&P 500 (SP500) stock remains above historical averages and close to the highest level since 2019. “As a result of these positioning dynamics, market breadth remains extremely narrow, a condition that typically signals risk to intra-market momentum,” Snider said.

The increased investor risk appetite has also boosted equity capital markets activity significantly. The strategist noted that the median U.S. IPO (IPO) in June rose by 37% in its first trading day, representing the “best month since early 2024 and a top decile return relative to the past three decades.”

SPAC activity has also surged, with the second quarter of 2025 being the largest for SPAC activity since the first quarter of 2022, raising a total of $9B and contributing to a first-half total of $12B.

data.title: Intel tumbles as it continues to burn money, lose market share to AMD: analysts
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares tumbled during early market trading on Friday as the semiconductor firm continues to find its footing in foundry while losing market share to rivals such as AMD (NASDAQ:AMD).

The Santa Clara, Calif.-based Intel reported its second quarter results and outlook on Thursday evening. Revenue exceeded market expectations, while earnings per share were weaker than the consensus due in part to restructuring charges. Shares were down more than 8% by Friday morning. This followed a 3.6% decline on Thursday.

Analysts surmised that tariff pull-ins contributed in part to the stronger than expected revenue as Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan continues to try and turn the company around.

"One of the most important aspects is that Lip-Bu has finally made a decisive move regarding Intel's IDM/foundry strategy by stating that they will not proceed with the next gen 14A manufacturing node (and future nodes) without securing a significant external foundry customer - potentially signaling a move to a more fabless model in 3-4 years," said J.P. Morgan analysts Harlan Sur and Peter Peng, in a Friday note. "While we view this as a positive step, customer decisions on 14A node adoption won’t be made for another 18-24 months. In the meantime, Intel will continue to burn cash and concede more market share in the PC client and server business to AMD."

J.P. Morgan identifies near-term risks, such as customer pull-in purchases falling off and gross margins continuing to drop below 40%. The bank reiterated its underweight rating on the stock but nudged the price target up to $21 from $20.

Morgan Stanley also said that gross margins will remain under pressure due to the effects of 18A startup costs and the Lunar Lake ramp.

"Both of those headwinds seem likely to persist into next year, and should the PC market soften that and lower volumes won't be a pleasant combination," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, in a Friday note. "Those prospects seem likely to temper enthusiasm for any near-term V-shaped fundamental recovery even with the company reporting its first positive y/y revenue quarter since Q1 2024."

Morgan Stanley retained its Equal-weight rating and $23 price target following the results.

Meanwhile, KeyBanc analysts said they were encouraged by the results and the potential for a turnaround. The bank has assigned a Sector Weight on the stock and no price target.

"All things considered, we're encouraged by these results, and are modestly raising '26E despite lowering '25E," said KeyBanc analysts John Vinh and Ryan Rosumny, in an investor note. "However, we remain SW, as we expect a potential turnaround to take some time."

Price action from competitors was mixed on Friday. AMD had inched up 0.5%, and Nvidia (NVDA) was down 0.4%.

data.title: Valaris adds $1B-plus of new backlog in latest fleet status report
data.description:
Valaris (NYSE:VAL) said it has added more than $1B of new backlog in the past three months and was awarded contract extensions for three of its jackups, the company said in its latest fleet status report.

Valaris (NYSE:VAL) said it won a four-year contract extension valued at $117M for the Valaris 110 jackup offshore Qatar, which is expected to begin in October 2025 in direct continuation of the existing contract.

The company said the Valaris Norway jackup was awarded a 150-day contract extension valued at $18M by Ithaca Energy for work in the U.K. North Sea, which is set to begin in February 2026 in direct continuation of the existing contract.

The final deal was a 31-day contract extension valued at $3.5M with two 28-day priced options for the Valaris 122 jackup with Shell in the U.K. North Sea, which is expected to begin in December 2025 in direct continuation of the existing contract.

However, a contract with Harbour Energy for the Valaris 120 jackup has been suspendedstarting in September 2025, while a contract with Anasuria Hibiscus for the Valaris 248 jackup was terminated by mutual agreement.

Valaris (VAL) also agreed to sell the Valaris 247 jackup for cash proceeds of $108M.

data.title: Wall Street opens up day after S&P 500, Nasdaq touch fresh closing highs
data.description:
The stock market opened higher on Friday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite hit fresh record high closes. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.1%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was flat. 

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.92%.

Durable goods dropped less than feared in June, with new orders for manufactured durable goods: -9.3% M/M to $311.8B vs. -10.4% consensus and +16.5% prior (revised from +16.4%)

Wall Street expressed a cautious tone on Thursday, with the major averages closing mixed as investors weighed a wave of corporate earnings—highlighted by results from Alphabet and Tesla—against rising Treasury yields following an unexpected fall in jobless claims.

"The risk-on tone just about continued yesterday, as another batch of strong U.S. data supported investor optimism, with the S&P 500 ending a quiet session at a 4th consecutive all-time high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

On the earnings end, Intel (INTC) shares were down 5% in premarket trade after the company's bottom line in the second quarter missed Wall Street estimates. The firm's Q3 outlook also failed to impress investors. The company also announced a 15% workforce reduction. 

Phillips 66 (PSX) will release its quarterly results on Friday during market hours.

data.title: KalVista, Biogen, Ionis get EU positive opinions for key drugs
data.description:
KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV), Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB), and Ionis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:IONS) announced that the European Medicines Agency (EMA) has given positive endorsements for their products.

The EMA's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended marketing authorization for KalVista's Sebetralstat, an oral medication designed to treat acute attacks of hereditary angioedema in adults and adolescents aged 12 and older.

A final decision from the European Commission for Sebetralstat is anticipated by early October.

Additionally, the CHMP has supported Biogen and Sage Therapeutics' (NASDAQ:SAGE) Zurzuvae for treating postpartum depression in adults after childbirth. The EC final decision expected in the third quarter of 2025.

The CHMP has also endorsed Ionis' Tryngolza as a dietary adjunct for adult patients suffering from genetically confirmed familial chylomicronemia syndrome. This positive opinion will be reviewed by EC for approval, with a decision expected by the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: Apple iSports to acquire LBC Enterprises Pty Ltd
data.description:
• Apple iSports (OTCPK:AAPI) has signed a definitive agreement to acquire LBC Enterprises Pty, an online gaming, wagering, and payment systems company.
  • The Board of Directors of both LBC and Apple iSports have unanimously approved the proposed transaction, which is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025.
  • LBC will continue to operate under its brand and will be led by Ian Parke, the CEO of LBC.

data.title: Meritage Homes outlines double-digit community growth for 2025 and 2026 as company recalibrates land spend
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Meritage Homes Corporation (MTH) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Phillippe Lord highlighted that Meritage achieved "312 active stores, the highest community count in company history with more planned growth to come in the second half of the year." Lord emphasized the company's ability to "reduce our construction time from approximately 120 calendar days in the first quarter of 2025 to about 110 this quarter," which supports maintaining optimal inventory and absorption rates even amid volatile market conditions.
  • Management stated that Q2 2025 saw the delivery of 4,170 homes and the generation of home closing revenue of $1.6 billion, with an adjusted home closing gross margin of 21.4% (excluding terminated land deal charges). Diluted EPS for the quarter was $2.04, and book value per share increased 10% year-over-year.
  • CFO Hilla Sferruzza stated, "Second quarter 2025 home closing revenue of $1.6 billion was 5% lower compared to prior year despite a 1% increase in closing volume, primarily as a result of increased utilization of financing incentives, which drove our ASP on closings lower to $387,000 per home."
  • Lord noted the company is "intentionally toggling between our spend on land and a focus on shareholder returns," and cited a disciplined approach to land acquisition, with approximately 1,800 lots terminated in Q2 due to changing criteria.

Outlook

  • Sferruzza provided Q3 2025 guidance: "For Q3 2025, we are projecting total home closings between 3,600 and 3,900 units, home closing revenue of $1.4 billion to $1.56 billion, home closing gross margin of around 20%, an effective tax rate of about 24.5% and diluted EPS in the range of $1.51 to $1.86."
  • Management reiterated expectations for "double-digit year-over-year growth for our 2025 year-end community count," with Lord commenting that "we expect another solid year of double-digit growth between the beginning of that year and the end of 2026."
  • There is no full-year guidance due to "volatility in the market at this time and our high backlog conversion," which limits visibility beyond the next quarter.

Financial Results

  • Home closing revenue for Q2 2025 was $1.6 billion, down 5% year-over-year, driven by a lower average selling price due to increased financing incentives.
  • Adjusted home closing gross margin was 21.4% (excluding $4.2 million in terminated land deal charges).
  • SG&A as a percentage of home closing revenue increased to 10.2%, up from 9.3% a year ago, attributed to higher commissions, startup costs for new divisions, and increased spec inventory carrying costs.
  • The company returned $76 million in cash to shareholders in Q2 2025, including $45 million in share buybacks and $31 million in cash dividends. Year-to-date, share buybacks totaled $90 million, reducing the share count by almost 2%.
  • Land acquisition and development spend was reduced to $509 million in Q2 2025, a 12% decrease from the prior year, with full-year land spend target lowered from $2.5 billion to $2 billion.

Q&A

  • Trevor Scott Allinson, Wolfe Research: Asked about absorption rates on new communities. Lord responded that "they opened up and met our expectations as it relates to what we were thinking we were going to get from those communities."
  • Alan S. Ratner, Zelman & Associates: Inquired about volume outlook and spec starts. Lord confirmed the company is "slowing down starts because of our cycle times" but continuing to ramp up starts in new communities. On capital allocation, Sferruzza said, "we're definitely going to be pressing on the gas on the repurchases while kind of just keeping an eye on the store."
  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Asked about margin impact from new community openings. Lord explained, "we always traditionally try to open up a community and gain momentum. Usually the #1 lever to do that is pricing."
  • John Lovallo, UBS: Questioned margin trajectory into Q3 and Q4. Sferruzza stated, "the majority of the pullback that you're seeing is loss leverage," and clarified no guidance was given for Q4.
  • Michael Jason Rehaut, JPMorgan: Probed the decision to pull full-year guidance. Lord said it was due to "the lack of visibility we have right now with our current backlog," while Sferruzza added, "We don't even have all of our closings yet for Q3. We have 0 closings identified yet for Q4."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a cautious to slightly negative tone, pressing for clarity on volume outlook, margin trends, and the rationale for withdrawing full-year guidance. Questions focused on how the company plans to maintain growth and profitability given market headwinds.
  • Management maintained a pragmatic and disciplined tone, emphasizing agility and a focus on what can be controlled. Prepared remarks expressed confidence, while Q&A responses were measured, often referencing market volatility and limited visibility. Direct quotes such as "we believe our strategy was designed to provide certainty and weather these challenges head on" (CEO Lord) and "I don't think we're going to have any discussion about that" (CFO Sferruzza, regarding Q4 guidance) reflected both confidence and caution.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed increased caution, with a notable shift from reiterating annual guidance to providing only quarterly projections.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q2 2025 in the previous call included full-year projections; the current quarter provides only Q3 guidance, reflecting increased uncertainty.
  • Land spend was recalibrated, lowering the full-year target to $2 billion from $2.5 billion, in response to economic conditions.
  • Community count grew from 290 at Q1-end to 312 at Q2-end, reflecting execution on planned expansion.
  • Gross margin and EPS both declined compared to Q1, with management attributing this to higher incentives, increased SG&A, and loss leverage.
  • Management's tone shifted from confidence in maintaining full-year guidance to a more conservative stance, citing limited visibility.
  • Analysts increased the frequency and depth of questions on risk management, capital allocation, and the sustainability of community count growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited weakened consumer sentiment and continued volatility in mortgage rates as primary business risks.
  • Elevated use of financing incentives and persistent affordability challenges in the entry-level segment were noted as ongoing headwinds.
  • Analysts expressed concern about the impact of rising resale inventory, margin compression, and the company's ability to deliver growth without full-year guidance.
  • The company is mitigating risk by recalibrating land spend, maintaining a flexible cost structure, and focusing on disciplined capital allocation and shareholder returns.

Final Takeaway

Meritage Homes underscored its adaptability in a volatile housing market by expanding its community count to a company record, accelerating inventory turnover, and recalibrating capital deployment to prioritize shareholder returns. While market headwinds led to a reduction in land spend and a withdrawal of full-year guidance, management remains committed to double-digit community count growth in both 2025 and 2026 and aims to navigate the current environment through operational agility and a disciplined approach to investment and risk.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
